question: Explain the difference between a combinational logic circuit and a sequential logic circuit. Provide an example of each.
expectedPoints: Combinational circuits: output depends solely on current input values (no memory)
Sequential circuits: output depends on current inputs and previous state (has memory elements like flip-flops)
Example of sequential: Counter or register

question: Describe the fetch-decode-execute cycle and explain how it is fundamental to computer operation.
expectedPoints: Fetch: instruction retrieved from memory address in PC
Decode: instruction interpreted by control unit
Execute: operation performed (ALU, memory access, etc.)
Basis of von Neumann architecture operation

question: Compare and contrast RISC and CISC architectures, discussing their design philosophies, instruction characteristics, and relative advantages in modern computing.
expectedPoints: RISC: Reduced Instruction Set Computer - simpler instructions, load-store architecture
CISC: Complex Instruction Set Computer - richer instruction set, memory-memory operations
RISC advantages: pipelining efficiency, simpler hardware design
CISC advantages: code density, complex operations in single instruction
Modern convergence: RISC-like internal execution in CISC processors
